name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value strictly greater than the original input

name: align_identity_broken
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_identity_broken(a: usize)
    requires
        a < usize::MAX,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, 1) != a,
{
}
reason: Aligning to a 1-byte boundary should be the identity operation for any input

name: zero_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_3_zero_not_aligned(b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(0 as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: Zero is divisible by every positive number and should always be considered aligned

name: aligned_not_fixpoint
code:
proof fn phi_4_aligned_not_fixpoint(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: If an address is already aligned, align_to_usize should return it unchanged

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The result of align_to_usize must itself be aligned to the given boundary
